project.name: Clawvato
project.description: Business operating system for solo technical founders — memory-powered AI agent across Slack, email, calendar, Drive, meetings, and Claude Code

B.name: Slack MCP + Agent Core
B.notes: Hybrid agent active. Old monolith removed.

C.name: Google Workspace
C.notes: 12 fast-path tools (calendar, gmail read/search, drive read/search with PDF support, freebusy). gws CLI for deep path.

D.name: Memory + Embeddings
D.notes: Dynamic categories (16 seed + discovered). Entity junction table. Path-aware budgets. Relevance-based retrieval. Async findings file. FTS5-based consolidation.

F.name: Security Hardening
F.notes: Priority 1 mitigations done (subprocess env allowlist, response sanitization, findings file randomization). Priority 2-3 in docs/MITIGATION_PLAN.md.

H.name: Plugin Adapter + CC Bridge
H.notes: Full design at docs/DESIGN_PLUGIN_ADAPTER.md. Enables CC sessions to share memory with Slack agent.

I.name: Fireflies.ai
I.notes: GraphQL client + CLI wrapper. Search + summary on fast path.

J.name: Hybrid Architecture (fast + deep)
J.notes: Renamed heavy→deep. Fast path expanded to 12 tools. Pre-flight conversation for deep path. Async findings file for memory capture.

infrastructure.googleAuth: gws CLI config via GWS_CONFIG_B64 env var
infrastructure.maxPlan: Claude Max subscription — deep path costs $0

knownIssues.title: FTS5 query sanitization — special chars cause silent fallback. See docs/MITIGATION_PLAN.md
hasVectorSupport probe per call — no cache
Pre-flight loop has no hard wall-time timeout
No tests for agent orchestration layer (hybrid, fast-path, handler)

backlog.title: Daily/weekly briefing — scheduled morning summary of email, calendar, commitments, follow-ups
Plugin adapter + unified memory bridge — CC sessions share memory with Slack agent. Design: docs/DESIGN_PLUGIN_ADAPTER.md
--session support for deep path — maintain continuity across Slack thread messages
Commitment tracking + follow-up reminders — wire extracted commitments to proactive alerts
GitHub integration — PRs, issues, CI status awareness
Finance integration — invoicing, bookkeeping, A/P, contract payment schedules (QuickBooks/Stripe plugin)

currentSprint.name: Memory Refactor + UX Overhaul
goals: Rename heavy→deep + expand fast path (3→12 tools) ✅
Bug fixes: stale progress, interrupt buffer, timer leak ✅
Memory refactor: dynamic categories, entity junction, path-aware budgets ✅
Async findings file (zero overhead during research) ✅
Drive file content reading (PDFs, docs) on fast path ✅

lastSession.type: Memory refactor + UX overhaul + security hardening
keyDecisions: Dynamic memory categories — seed + discovered organically
Path-aware budgets: fast (1500 tokens) vs deep (50000 tokens)
Async findings file: Opus writes one JSON dump at end, background processor stores
Pre-flight is LLM-driven conversation, not regex matching
Subprocess env switched to allowlist (Slack tokens excluded)
Project vision expanded: business OS, not just chief of staff
Engineering philosophy: always take the pain now, build for durability

thingsNotToRedo: DON'T REBUILD WHAT COMES NATIVELY WITH CLAUDE
Don't hardcode prompts in TypeScript — all prompts in config/prompts/*.md
Don't hardcode tunables in TypeScript — all operational values in config/default.json
Always take the pain now — build for durability, no short-term hacks
Bug reports → investigate + plan + confirm before coding
MCP stdio servers MUST redirect ALL logging to stderr
Deep path subprocess env: ALLOWLIST only, never pass Slack tokens
FTS5 triggers get corrupted when renaming tables — drop triggers first, rebuild after
Per-finding MCP store_fact calls add 2-5s each — use async findings file instead
Findings file must use UUID-stamped path (no fixed /tmp/ path — race condition)
SQLite CHECK constraint removal requires table rebuild + FTS5 trigger rebuild
RESET_DB env var in docker-entrypoint.sh for emergency DB wipe on Railway
